Name: rate_distortion_compression
Title: Information-Theoretic Limits of Neural Network Compression: A Rate-Distortion Approach
Short Hypothesis: We hypothesize that the fundamental limits of neural network compression can be characterized using rate-distortion theory, and that this approach can provide a principled framework for balancing compression rate with task-relevant accuracy.
Related Work: Recent works such as [1], [2], and [8] have explored the application of rate-distortion theory to neural network compression. However, these works have focused primarily on empirical evaluations and have not provided a comprehensive theoretical framework for understanding the information-theoretic limits of neural network compression. Our proposal builds on this existing research by providing a thorough analysis of the rate-distortion tradeoff in neural network compression and exploring the implications of this tradeoff for compression algorithm design.
Abstract: Neural network compression is a critical component of efficient AI systems, but existing methods often rely on ad-hoc heuristics and lack a principled framework for balancing compression rate with task-relevant accuracy. In this proposal, we explore the application of rate-distortion theory to neural network compression, with the goal of characterizing the fundamental limits of compression and providing a principled framework for compression algorithm design. We will analyze the rate-distortion tradeoff in neural network compression and explore the implications of this tradeoff for compression algorithm design. Our approach will provide a comprehensive theoretical framework for understanding the information-theoretic limits of neural network compression and will have significant implications for the design of efficient AI systems.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Analyze the rate-distortion tradeoff in neural network compression using a variety of compression algorithms and datasets.
Experiment 2: Evaluate the performance of compression algorithms designed using rate-distortion theory and compare to existing state-of-the-art methods.
Experiment 3: Explore the implications of the rate-distortion tradeoff for compression algorithm design and evaluate the performance of algorithms designed using this framework.
Risk Factors and Limitations: The complexity of the rate-distortion tradeoff in neural network compression may make it difficult to analyze and optimize.
The performance of compression algorithms designed using rate-distortion theory may not generalize to all datasets and tasks.
The computational resources required to optimize compression algorithms using rate-distortion theory may be significant.
